rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Motion To Bifurcate Trial On The Issue Of Medical Causation
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 17, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant BorgWarner Morse Tec LLC's motion to bifurcate trial on the issue of medical causation is denied without prejudice. The motion may be raised before the trial judge. Although defendant's moving papers do not comply with C.C.P. § 1008, defendant did demonstrate new or different facts or circumstances (action no longer has trial date) that would justify the Court considering this motion, the second motion defendant has brought seeking this same relief. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Douglass Insulation Companys Joinder To Defendant Borgwarner Morse Tec Inc, As Successor-By-Merger To Borg-Warner Corporations Motion To Bifurcate Trial On The Issue Of Medical Causation
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 17, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Douglass Insulation Company's joinder to BorgWarner Morse Tec LLC's motion to bifurcate trial on the issue of medical causation is denied without prejudice. The underlying motion may be raised before the trial judge. Although BorgWarner's moving papers do not comply with C.C.P. § 1008, BorgWarner did demonstrate new or different facts or circumstances (action no longer has trial date) that would justify the Court considering the underlying motion, the second motion moving defendant has brought seeking this same relief. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ERNEST MOUTON (PROVIDE ACCESS) VS. GOODRICH CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion For Appointment Of Counsel (To Be Heard At The Trial Setting Conference In Dept 206)(See "Notes")
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 17, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Plaintiff Ernest Mouton, Jr.'s motion for appointment of counsel: hearing required. The hearing will be at 2:30p.m. in conjunction with the Trial Setting Conference calendar. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)
